topic_title: The Judgment Framework — Q2: Can I verify this without the AI?

positional_bias.note: Positions 1 and 3 each hold 2/6 = 33.3%, well under the 40% cap. No shuffle needed.

per_question.note: Correct is not the longest — distractor 1 (182 chars) exceeds it; net flag = false.

notes: q1: rephrasing=independence, timing gap=update, different wording=different reasoning — all documented misconceptions.
q2: verification=production (Section 5.2 names this confusion), human-written docs as disqualifier, partial-unless-rewritten — plausible.
q3: ask-AI-to-explain, consensus-across-models, confidence-as-signal — documented automation-bias failure modes.
q4: disclaimer as mitigation, impossibility as permission, retroactive Q1 revision — plausible under pressure.
q5: low-cost assumption, over-checking charge, automation-delegation fallacy — plausible.
q6: difficult vs impossible conflation is exactly the LO4 discriminator; temporary unavailability vs fundamental impossibility is the second axis — genuine learner confusions.

coverage_map.lo: LO6 — apply Q2 to real scenario

notes: All stems use plain language. Q1/Q2 referenced contextually with meaning established by the stem or corpus vocabulary.
q6 correctly targets the LO4 straightforward/difficult/impossible taxonomy without referencing any un-introduced concept.
No question requires knowledge beyond this topic's corpus.

verdict: PASS — all five checks pass on cycle 2. LO4 (recognise straightforward / difficult / genuinely impossible verification scenarios) is now covered by q6. Positional distribution is balanced (33% max share, under 40% cap). No length-bias flag: in q6 the correct answer is shorter than the longest distractor. Distractor plausibility is high across all six questions. Full LO coverage achieved.
